challenges.title: HTML Form Builder
challenges.description: Write a function `build_form(fields, action='/submit', method='POST')` that takes a dict of field names to field types (e.g., {'username': 'text', 'password': 'password'}) and returns an HTML form string. Each field should have a label and input element. The form should include a submit button.
challenges.starter_code:
def build_form(fields, action='/submit', method='POST'):
    """Generate an HTML form string from a dict of field_name: input_type.
    
    Returns a string containing a <form> with labeled inputs and a submit button.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass
challenges.test_code:
html = build_form({'name': 'text'})
assert '<form' in html
assert 'action="/submit"' in html
assert 'method="POST"' in html
assert '<input' in html
assert 'type="text"' in html
assert 'name="name"' in html
assert 'submit' in html.lower()
html2 = build_form({'email': 'email', 'pw': 'password'}, action='/login', method='GET')
assert 'action="/login"' in html2
assert 'method="GET"' in html2
assert 'type="email"' in html2
assert 'name="email"' in html2
assert 'type="password"' in html2
assert 'name="pw"' in html2
empty = build_form({})
assert '<form' in empty
assert 'submit' in empty.lower()
hints: Build the HTML string piece by piece: start with <form>, add <label> and <input> for each field, add a submit button, and close with </form>.
Use f-strings to insert the field name and type into the HTML elements.

challenges.title: Form Validator
challenges.description: Write a `FormValidator` class that validates form data. Use `add_required(field_name)` to mark fields as required. Use `add_rule(field_name, validator_func, error_msg)` to add custom validation. The `validate(data)` method takes a dict of field values and returns a dict: {'valid': bool, 'errors': {field: [error_messages]}}.
challenges.starter_code:
class FormValidator:
    """Validates form data against required fields and custom rules."""

    def __init__(self):
        # Your code here
        pass

    def add_required(self, field_name):
        """Mark a field as required."""
        # Your code here
        pass

    def add_rule(self, field_name, validator_func, error_msg):
        """Add a custom validation rule for a field.
        
        validator_func takes the field value and returns True if valid.
        """
        # Your code here
        pass

    def validate(self, data):
        """Validate data dict. Returns {'valid': bool, 'errors': dict}."""
        # Your code here
        pass
challenges.test_code:
v = FormValidator()
v.add_required('username')
v.add_required('email')
v.add_rule('email', lambda x: '@' in x, 'Must contain @')
v.add_rule('username', lambda x: len(x) >= 3, 'Must be at least 3 chars')
result = v.validate({'username': 'alice', 'email': 'alice@test.com'})
assert result['valid'] is True
assert result['errors'] == {}
result2 = v.validate({'username': 'al', 'email': 'bad'})
assert result2['valid'] is False
assert 'username' in result2['errors']
assert 'email' in result2['errors']
assert 'Must be at least 3 chars' in result2['errors']['username']
assert 'Must contain @' in result2['errors']['email']
result3 = v.validate({'username': 'bob'})
assert result3['valid'] is False
assert 'email' in result3['errors']
result4 = v.validate({})
assert result4['valid'] is False
assert 'username' in result4['errors']
assert 'email' in result4['errors']
hints: Store required fields in a set and rules in a dict mapping field names to lists of (validator_func, error_msg) tuples.
In validate(), first check required fields exist and are non-empty, then run custom rules. Collect all errors per field.
